description: The metallic element iron is 'tesu'. It is worked in many forms, reacts readily with oxygen and moisture, and supplies the main metal in 'teshilo' (steel). Strength depends on composition and treatment rather than following from the iron name alone.
articulatory_notes: The tongue tip closes behind the upper teeth for dental 't' and releases into stressed front 'e'. A narrow groove along the tongue directs unvoiced 's' into rounded high-back 'u'.
usage_notes: Names such as ore, cast iron, or wrought iron preserve how the material was obtained and worked. 'tesu' supplies the iron common to the account.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Iron changes under furnace heat, hammering, cooling, and exposure to water. The craft lies in reading those changes rather than assuming that every dark metal will answer alike.

semantic_domains.creation: a metal worked into tools, structures, and other made forms
semantic_domains.physical: a reactive metallic element and the main constituent of many ferrous materials
